data.title: Cardano: Deep Dive on the Trump Reserve Token Whose Blockchain Ignores TVL
data.description:
• Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard told CoinDesk that he measures real-world use cases as an adoption metric and aims for fewer than 50% of on-chain "value transactions."
  • Cardano's ADA token was mentioned by U.S. president Donald Trump as one of the tokens that would make up the nation's crypto reserve.
  • Gregaard also explained the financial relationship between the Cardano Foundation, Emurgo and Charles Hoskinson's IOG.

data.title: Bitcoin Tops $84K, Battling Key Level for Bulls; SOL, LINK Lead Gains
data.description:
• Cryptocurrencies rebounded on Friday, with BTC climbing to $85,000. Chainlink's LINK, Solana's SOL, and SUI led gains in the broad-market CoinDesk 20 Index.
  • Risk appetite returned to traditional markets as well, lifting the S&P 500 and Nasdaq indexes up 1.7% and 2.3%, respectively. Gold backed off from its fresh record above $3,000.
  • Well-followed trader Bob Loukas said stocks and BTC have more room to run in the next weeks, recovering from oversold levels.

data.title: Bitcoin-Related Convertible Bond ETF Comes to Market
data.description:
• REX Shares launched the first ETF focused on convertible bonds tied to bitcoin-focused corporate treasury strategies.
  • The convertible paper of Michael Saylor's Strategy makes up the majority of the fund holdings.
  • Trading on Nasdaq with a 0.85% expense ratio, the fund came to market on Friday with $25 million in AUM.

data.title: Euler Looks to Build on V2's DeFi Lending Comeback Story
data.description:
• Euler nearly closed shop after suffering a $200 million hack in March 2023.
  • The lending platform's team decided instead to rebuild Euler from scratch.
  • Now, the platform's setting new all-time highs in key metrics.

data.title: Russia Turns to Crypto to Bypass Western Sanctions in Oil Trade: Reuters
data.description:
• Russian oil companies reportedly use bitcoin, ether, and USDT to convert Chinese yuan and Indian rupees into roubles.
  • The crypto transactions account for a small but growing share of Russia’s $192 billion oil trade.
  • Traditional currencies like the UAE dirham remain the dominant payment method.

data.title: ZKsync Sunsets Liquidity Rewards Program, Citing Bearish Market Conditions
data.description:
• Citing bearish market conditions, ZKsync will sunset its liquidity rewards program.
  • TVL on ZKsync has dropped by around 50% since Jan. 30 and the ZK token has lost 35% of its value in the past month.
  • ZKsync will now be shifting focus to the Elastic Network, which is comprised of multiple chains within the ZKsync ecosystem.

data.title: Gold's Historic Rally Leaves Bitcoin Behind, But the Trend May Reverse
data.description:
• Gold ETFs saw $10 billion in inflows over the past 30 days, while bitcoin ETFs recorded $5 billion in outflows, a divergence expected to reverse, according to ByTree Founder, Charlie Morris.
  • Spot gold reaches $3,002, up over 15% this year, fueled by ETF inflows, geopolitical risks, and U.S. tariff concerns.

data.title: S&P 500 Enters Correction Territory, What Does This Mean For Bitcoin?
data.description:
• The S&P 500 has entered correction territory, down 10% from its all-time high, with past 20% corrections occurring in 2009, 2019, 2020, and 2022.
  • Bitcoin has fallen 30% from its peak, a typical pattern in past bull market corrections, including the most recent one in August 2024 during the yen carry trade unwind.

data.title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial (WLFI) Completes $590M Token Sale
data.description:
• World Liberty Financial (WLFI), a crypto project backed by Donald Trump, has successfully closed its token sale, raising approximately $590 million.
  • The project's co-founder, Zak Folkman, credited Tron's Justin Sun for the success of the token sale, following Sun's investment of $30 million.
  • The WLFI token was only available to accredited investors and cannot be transferred or publicly sold on exchanges, with no set date for an exchange listing.

data.title: AI's Lead Over Crypto for VC Dollars Increased in Q1'25, But Does This Race Really Matter?
data.description:
• In the first quarter of 2025, crypto venture funding in the U.S. reached approximately $861 million, but was significantly outpaced by nearly $20 billion in artificial intelligence (AI) funding.
  • Notable AI deals included Databricks' $15.3 billion round and Anthropic's $2 billion raise, while the largest crypto deal was a $2 billion investment into Binance by Abu Dhabi's MGX.
  • Historical data indicates a consistent preference for AI over crypto in venture capital funding, with AI funding growing from $670 million in 2011 to $36 billion in 2020.
